claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start by gathering some information about your circumstances.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions. Child Benefit eligibility is determined separately for each child, so I'll need information about each child you're inquiring about.

Let me start with the first question:

**Do you have a child (or children) that you're responsible for? If yes, how old are they?**

Please tell me the age of each child you'd like to check eligibility for.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I'd like to check if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex, who's 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! You have a son, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is between 16 and 19 years old, his eligibility depends on whether he's in approved education or training. 

**Is Alex currently in approved education or training?**

This typically includes:
- Full-time non-advanced education (such as A-levels or Scottish Highers at school or college)
- Approved vocational training programs

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school but hasn't gone on to do anything else.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, I need to ask one more important question:

**When did Alex leave education?** 

This is important because there may be an extension period that applies if he left education recently (within the last 20 weeks).

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He left school a few months back - it was when the school year ended last summer.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Since Alex left school last summer (a few months ago), I need to determine if he falls within the 20-week extension period that can apply when a young person aged 16-17 leaves education.

**After Alex left education, did he register with any of the following services:**
- A government careers service
- Connexions (a careers advisory service)
- The armed services

This registration would allow Child Benefit to continue for up to 20 weeks after leaving education, or until he turns 18, whichever comes first.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he hasn't registered with any of those services. He's just been at home trying to figure out what he wants to do next.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Based on what you've told me, I now have enough information to assess Alex's eligibility for Child Benefit.

Let me confirm the key points:
- Alex is 17 years old
- He is not currently in approved education or training
- He left school last summer (several months ago)
- He has not registered with a government careers service, Connexions, or the armed services

**My Assessment:**

Unfortunately, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

**The reason:** For young people aged 16-19, Child Benefit only continues if they are in approved education or training. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, Child Benefit cannot be paid.

There is a 20-week extension period available for 16-17 year olds who have recently left education, but this only applies if they register with a government careers service, Connexions, or the armed services. Since Alex has not registered with any of these services, the extension does not apply.

**What this means:** If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it would have stopped when he left education (or shortly after, depending on when you notified the Child Benefit Office).

**What could change this:** If Alex starts approved education or training in the future (such as A-levels, T-levels, or an approved vocational course of at least 12 hours per week), you could become eligible for Child Benefit again at that point.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Thank you for explaining that. I understand now - since Alex isn't in education or training and hasn't registered with any of those services, I'm not eligible at the moment. I'll let him know about registering with the careers service if that might help, or if he decides to go back into education. That's all I needed to know, thanks for your help.
